title: Clear supports_ephemeral when WS upgrade fails permanently
description:
## Why this issue exists

After the 8lgk epic, `supports_ephemeral` is set from `ws_url.is_some()` at session bootstrap. If the server correctly advertises both `urn:ietf:params:jmap:websocket` and `urn:ietf:params:jmap:chat:websocket` but the WebSocket upgrade subsequently fails on every retry (proxy strips Upgrade headers, port blocked, TLS mismatch), the flag remains `true` indefinitely. The UI renders the typing indicator area and presence badges, but they are permanently empty — the original silent-degradation problem reappears in a narrower failure mode.

## What needs to be done

1. Add `WsNotification::PermanentFailure` variant to the WS notification enum in `client_task.rs`. The WS subtask should send this after exhausting retries (e.g. N consecutive failures without a successful frame received), rather than continuing to loop with backoff forever.

2. In the main select! loop, when `WsNotification::PermanentFailure` is received, send `AppEvent::EphemeralUnavailable` to the UI.

3. Add `AppEvent::EphemeralUnavailable` to `event.rs`. The `apply_event` handler in `app.rs` sets `self.supports_ephemeral = false`.

4. Add a tracing::warn! at the permanent-failure point: `"WebSocket upgrade failed after N retries; disabling ephemeral events"`.

## Acceptance criteria

- After N consecutive WS upgrade failures, `AppState.supports_ephemeral` becomes `false`
- UI stops rendering typing/presence elements after the flag clears
- SSE transport is unaffected throughout
- No panic paths; reconnect loop exits cleanly on permanent failure
- `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` pass

## Design notes

N (retry limit before declaring permanent failure) needs a decision. Options:
- Fixed count (e.g. 5 consecutive failures): simple, predictable
- Time-based (e.g. no success within 60s of first attempt): handles transient network blips better

Recommend fixed count = 5 to start; can be made configurable later if needed.
